title: Translate to natural English
body:
Translate the following text into natural, conversational English. Preserve technical terms; rewrite anything that would sound awkward to a native speaker.

{{text}}

title: 翻译成简体中文
body:
把下面的内容翻译成简体中文。要求：自然口语化，保留专业术语，不要逐字直译。

{{text}}

title: Summarize a long article in 5 bullets
body:
Summarize the following article in 5 bullet points, then 1 takeaway sentence at the end.

{{article}}

title: Explain code in plain English
body:
Explain what this code does, line by line, in plain English a junior engineer would understand. Then list any subtle bugs or edge cases you notice.

```
{{code}}
```

title: Refactor for readability
body:
Refactor this code for readability. Keep behavior identical. Prefer named functions over comments. Output only the refactored code; explain only the non-obvious choices in 1-2 lines after.

```
{{code}}
```

title: Write a unit test for this function
body:
Write a unit test for the following function in {{language:string=Swift}}. Cover the happy path + 2 edge cases + 1 error case. Use the project's existing test framework.

```
{{function:multiline=}}
```

title: Code review like a senior
body:
Review the following code change as a senior {{language:string=Swift}} engineer would. Focus on: correctness, performance, error handling, naming, and any non-obvious concurrency / race-condition risks. Be terse — only flag what matters.

```
{{diff:multiline=}}
```

title: 邮件起草（专业语气）
body: Draft an email to {{recipient:string=}} about {{topic:string=}}. Tone: professional but warm. Constraints: ≤ 150 words, single CTA, no jargon, no thank-you-in-advance.

title: 邮件起草（中文商务）
body: 起草一封中文商务邮件给{{对象:string=}}，主题{{话题:string=}}。要求：正式但不生硬，控制在 200 字内，只有一个明确请求，不要客套话堆砌。

title: Brainstorm 10 angles
Job description → resume bullets
面试官提问准备
body:
我下周面试 {{position:string=}} 岗位。基于这份 JD（下方），列 10 道最可能问的技术 + 行为题，每题给一个简短答题骨架（不要展开）。

JD：{{jd:multiline=}}

title: Daily standup helper
body:
Turn my notes into a 3-line standup: yesterday / today / blockers. Trim filler. If a blocker has no owner, suggest one.

{{notes:multiline=}}

title: 周报生成（基于 git log）
body:
下面是我这周的 git commit 记录。把它整理成 5 条人能看的中文周报项，每项形式：[做了什么] → [带来什么影响]。隐去 commit hash 和分支名。

{{git_log:multiline=}}

title: OKR 拆解
body:
基于这个 Objective，拆出 3-4 个有数字、有时间、可验证的 Key Results。每个 KR 用「By [date], [metric] from X to Y」格式。

Objective: {{objective:string=}}

title: 决策框架（Pros/Cons + 推荐）
body:
我面前有这两个选择，帮我做结构化对比 + 给推荐：

选择 A：{{option_a:string=}}
选择 B：{{option_b:string=}}

输出格式：
1. 各 3 条最强 pro
2. 各 2 条最强 con
3. 一句话推荐 + 一句反对该推荐的最强反驳

title: ELI5 — explain like I'm 5
body: Explain {{concept:string=}} like I'm 5 years old. Use one analogy from everyday life. No jargon.

title: Feynman 学习法
body: 我刚学了 {{topic:string=}}。请扮演一个完全不懂这个主题的人，用质疑式提问帮我找出我哪里没真懂。每轮一个问题，等我答完再问下一个。

title: PDF / 长文 chunked 总结
body:
下面这段文档很长。请按主题分块总结，每块给：标题 + 3-5 句要点 + 1 句 "为什么这块重要"。如果检测到结构性错误（比如自相矛盾、逻辑断裂），单独标记。

{{document:multiline=}}

title: 对比同类产品
body:
对比 {{product_a:string=}} 和 {{product_b:string=}}：
1. 各自最强差异化（一句话）
2. 价格 / 订阅模型对比
3. 谁更适合 {{use_case:string=}}

来源最近半年内的公开信息；不确定的地方明确标注。

title: App Store description 优化
body:
下面是一段 App Store 描述。优化它：保留原意，但提高 conversion-readability — 第一句必须是一个具体场景钩子，bullet 不超过 6 条，每条 ≤ 12 字。

{{description:multiline=}}

title: ASO keyword research
body: Suggest 10 App Store keywords for an app that does {{description:string=}}. Each keyword should be: ≤ 12 chars, low competition, descriptive (not branded). Sort by estimated search volume desc.

title: Reddit Show & Tell post
body:
Draft a Reddit /r/{{subreddit:string=}} "Show & Tell" post for my project: {{project:string=}}.

Constraints:
- Title ≤ 70 chars, no clickbait, no emoji
- Body: lead with the constraint or non-obvious choice, not features
- End with one specific question to invite comments
- No links until paragraph 3

title: Twitter/X thread builder
body:
Turn this rough draft into a 6-tweet thread:
- Tweet 1: hook in ≤ 80 chars
- Tweets 2-5: one specific point each, all under 240 chars
- Tweet 6: links + soft CTA
- No emoji, no thread emojis (1/, 2/), no "buckle up"

Draft: {{draft:multiline=}}

title: Product Hunt 文案
body:
我准备把 {{product:string=}} 上 Product Hunt。生成：
1. Tagline（≤ 60 字符）
2. 一段 Description（≤ 260 字）
3. 第一条 Maker comment 的草稿（强调约束 / 非显性设计选择，不要列功能）

title: Coze / Dify 智能体 prompt 骨架
body:
我要在 {{platform:string=Coze}} 搭一个 {{role:string=}} 智能体。给我一个高质量 system prompt 起手：
- 角色定义（≤ 100 字）
- 能力边界（明确说不做什么）
- 输出格式约束（JSON / Markdown / 自然语言）
- 失败/不确定时的兜底
- 一段示例 input → output 演示

title: Claude Code 项目根 system prompt
body:
You are working in this repository as a senior {{language:string=Swift}} engineer. Conventions:
- Prefer editing existing files over creating new ones
- Match the codebase's existing style; don't introduce a new framework
- Run the project's tests after changes — the test command is `{{test_cmd:string=}}`
- Before each edit, read the file to understand the surrounding context
- Don't write throwaway comments; only WHY-comments

When given a task, plan briefly, then execute. State assumptions out loud only if non-obvious.

title: Claude Code 修 bug 输入模板
Cursor / Claude Code 实施新功能
body:
实施需求：{{feature:string=}}

约束：
- 不要为这一个功能搭新 abstraction，复用现有模式
- 改动落到尽量少的文件
- 边界情况写 unit test，不写集成测试
- 不删/重命名公共 API（除非必要并明确说出 breaking change）

title: Code review (senior peer)
body:
以资深 {{language:string=Swift}} 工程师视角 review 这段 diff。只 flag 真的影响：
1. 正确性 / data race / 内存安全
2. 性能（O(n^2)+ 或不必要 IO）
3. 名字误导 / 失效注释
4. 测试缺口（哪些路径未覆盖）
忽略风格 / 格式（让 linter 处理）。

```diff
{{diff:multiline=}}
```

title: Claude Code skill: changelog from git log
body:
下面是 git log 的 commit titles。整理成面向用户的 changelog（一段 2-3 行）：
- 用户能感知的功能 / 修复在前
- 内部重构、CI、依赖升级合并到末尾「Internal」段
- 不暴露 commit hash / 内部分支名

{{commit_log:multiline=}}

title: Cursor 项目记忆 .cursorrules
n8n + LLM 自动化提示词
body:
在 n8n workflow 中，我从 {{trigger:string=}} 拿到原始数据 {{input_shape:string=}}，需要 LLM 输出严格 JSON 形式：

{{json_schema:multiline=}}

要求：
- 只返回 JSON，不要 markdown 代码块包裹
- 数值字段不要带单位字符串
- 缺失字段返回 null 而不是空字符串
- 任何字段不确定都返回 null + 在 "_uncertain" 字段列出

title: MCP server 设计模板
body:
为 Claude Code / Cursor 设计 MCP server，封装 {{capability:string=}}。

需要给我输出：
1. server 名字 + 一句话定位
2. 暴露的 tool 列表（每个：name / description / inputSchema）
3. 安全边界（哪些操作绝不暴露）
4. 错误处理：API 失败时返回什么 shape
5. 需要的环境变量 + 是否敏感（API key 类）
6. 一个完整 example tool call 演示

title: Claude Code PR Review
body:
Review this PR for {{language:string=Swift}} code. Focus on {{focus:string=correctness, performance, naming}}.

Diff:
{{diff:multiline=}}

Provide:
1. Critical issues (blockers)
2. Suggestions (non-blocking)
3. Praise (what's done well)

title: Swift async/await refactor
body:
Refactor the following Swift code from completion-handler or Combine style to async/await + structured concurrency.

Requirements:
- Use `async throws` where errors are possible
- Replace DispatchQueue.main.async with `@MainActor`
- Cancel on Task cancellation (check `Task.isCancelled` in loops)
- Keep the public API signature backward-compatible if called from Obj-C

```swift
{{code:multiline=}}
```

title: iOS crash report analyzer
body:
Analyze this iOS crash report and identify the root cause:

```
{{crash_report:multiline=}}
```

App version: {{version:string=}}
OS version: {{os_version:string=}}

Provide:
1. Crash type and thread that crashed
2. Exact function / line responsible
3. Likely cause (race condition, nil deref, OOM, etc.)
4. Minimal reproduction path
5. Recommended fix (with code snippet)

title: API 设计 review
body:
Review 这个 API 设计草稿。重点关注：

1. RESTful 一致性（资源命名、HTTP 动词使用）
2. 错误响应：4xx vs 5xx 划分对吗？错误体 schema 一致吗？
3. 幂等性：哪些端点应该 idempotent，是否做到
4. 分页：cursor-based 还是 offset？是否一致
5. 版本管理（URL vs Header）
6. 认证：哪些端点缺 auth？
7. 返回字段 deprecation 策略

设计：
{{api_spec:multiline=}}

title: AI 生成 SQL（带安全检查）
body:
需求：{{question:string=}}
表结构：
{{schema:multiline=}}

输出：
1. SQL 查询（{{dialect:string=PostgreSQL}}）
2. 解释每个 join / 子查询的意图
3. 警告（如适用）：
   - 这个 query 可能扫多少行？
   - 缺哪些索引会让它慢？
   - 是否有可能被 SQL injection（如果 user input 拼）
   - 数据量大时哪里要分页

title: AI 起 PR / MR 描述
body:
git diff 输出：
```
{{diff:multiline=}}
```

生成 PR 描述：
## What
（1-2 句话讲改动）

## Why
（动机：解决什么问题/实现什么需求）

## How
（关键设计选择 / 取舍，≤ 4 条 bullet）

## Test plan
（- [ ] 列表，每条具体到「跑什么命令验证什么行为」）

## Notes for reviewer
（如有：哪个文件先看 / 哪个改动反直觉）

title: AI 重写 commit message（按 conventional）
body:
把这条糟糕的 commit message 改成 conventional commits 格式：

原文：{{raw_message:string=}}

输出：
- 第一行：type(scope): subject（≤ 70 字符，subject 用祈使句）
- 空行
- body：解释 WHY（不是 WHAT，diff 已经说了 what）
- 如果是 breaking change，加 BREAKING CHANGE: 段

如果原文不够清晰，用 [需要补充] 标记，不编造细节。

title: 性能瓶颈定位 prompt
body:
下面是性能 profile / log 输出：

```
{{profile:multiline=}}
```

应用类型：{{app_type:string=iOS}}
问题症状：{{symptom:string=}}

分析：
1. 最可疑的 3 个瓶颈（按概率排序）
2. 每个瓶颈：怎么进一步验证？怎么修？
3. 不要的：CPU 100% 这种总览描述；要具体函数 / SQL / 网络调用
4. 末尾：low-risk quick win 1 个 + 高 leverage 但需重写的 1 个

title: AI 修生产环境 P0 bug
AI 起 deployment runbook
body:
我要部署 {{service:string=}} 到 production。

变更: {{deployment_changes:multiline=}}
依赖服务: {{dependencies:string=}}

请生成完整 deployment runbook:
1. **Pre-deploy checklist** (10-15 项)
2. **Deploy 步骤** (含每步预期时间 + 验证)
3. **Verification** (deploy 后 5 min / 30 min / 24h 检查)
4. **Rollback procedure** (准确 step + 时间)
5. **Smoke test commands**
6. **Monitoring dashboard 链接 + 关键指标**
7. **On-call 通知 template**
8. **Post-deploy retro questions**

title: AI 补全代码后的自我审查清单
body:
我刚让 AI 补完了一段 {{language:string=Swift}} 代码（如下）。在我合入前，按这清单 review：

1. 是否引入新的依赖 / import？是否必要？
2. 错误处理：边界情况覆盖？还是只有 happy path？
3. 命名是否符合本项目风格？
4. 性能：有 O(n^2)+ 或不必要 IO？
5. 安全：用户输入是否未校验直接拼接？
6. 测试：哪些路径还未覆盖？
7. 注释：是否有 AI 生成的废话注释？

代码：
```
{{code:multiline=}}
```

title: 代码迁移 prompt（语言 / 框架）
body:
把这段 {{from_lang:string=Objective-C}} 代码迁移到 {{to_lang:string=Swift}}。

```
{{code:multiline=}}
```

要求：
1. 保持语义等价；不重构
2. 保留原注释（翻译成 {{to_lang}} 习惯用法）
3. 标准库 / 第三方库映射用 {{to_lang}} 生态主流的（不要冷门）
4. 边界情况：原代码没显式处理的，新代码也别加（保持等价）
5. 末尾列出 3 个值得人类 review 的语义微妙点

title: 数据库 schema 演进 prompt
body:
我有这个 {{db_dialect:string=PostgreSQL}} schema：
```
{{current_schema:multiline=}}
```

需要支持新需求：{{requirement:string=}}

输出：
1. 推荐 schema 改动（DDL）
2. 数据迁移 SQL（生产安全的：分批、index 先建后删等）
3. 是否需要 application code 改动同步
4. 回滚方案
5. 性能影响估计（哪些 query 变慢/变快）
6. 建议的索引

title: Cursor Composer 新功能 multi-file edit
body:
用 Cursor Composer 做这个 multi-file change：

变更：{{change_description:string=}}
影响文件：{{files:string=}}

要求：
1. 跨文件保持 type 一致
2. 更新所有 caller
3. migration 脚本（如有 schema 变化）
4. 单测覆盖新 path
5. commit message 用 conventional commits
输出 git diff 格式

title: Blog post outline from notes
body:
Turn these rough notes into a structured blog post outline:

Topic: {{topic:string=}}
Target audience: {{audience:string=}}
Notes:
{{notes:multiline=}}

Output:
- Headline (3 options, A/B/C angle)
- Opening hook (2-sentence draft)
- 4-5 H2 sections with 2-3 bullet sub-points each
- Closing CTA
- Estimated word count

title: Sales copywriting (3 versions)
Story opening hook
body:
Write 5 different opening paragraphs for a story / essay about {{topic:string=}}.

Style: {{style:string=narrative}}
Tone: {{tone:string=introspective}}

Each opening should:
- Be ≤ 100 words
- Start in media res or with a concrete sensory detail
- NOT start with "In today's world" or any cliché preamble
- Create a distinct pull to keep reading

title: Technical documentation from code
body:
Write clear technical documentation for the following code:

```{{language:string=Swift}}
{{code:multiline=}}
```

Output:
1. One-sentence summary (what it does)
2. Parameters table (name / type / description / required)
3. Return value description
4. Usage example (copy-pasteable)
5. Edge cases and error conditions
6. "Don't use this when" section

title: LinkedIn post from insight
body:
Turn this raw insight into a LinkedIn post:

Insight: {{insight:string=}}
Context: {{context:string=}}

Requirements:
- First line must work as a standalone hook (no "I'm excited to share")
- 150-250 words total
- Short paragraphs (max 3 lines each)
- End with a single genuine question to invite comments
- No buzzwords: "game-changer", "thrilled", "humbled", "honored"

title: Newsletter issue from bullet notes
body:
Write a newsletter issue from these notes:

Topic: {{topic:string=}}
Notes: {{notes:multiline=}}
Tone: {{tone:string=conversational, slightly opinionated}}

Structure:
- Subject line (3 options)
- 1-line preview text
- Opening personal hook (50 words)
- 3 main sections (each: insight + 1 concrete example)
- One actionable takeaway
- Closing sign-off (≤ 30 words)

title: Customer case study
body:
Write a customer case study for {{company:string=}} using {{product:string=}}.

Facts:
- Problem before: {{before:string=}}
- Solution applied: {{solution:string=}}
- Results: {{results:string=}}

Format:
1. Headline (outcome-focused, ≤ 12 words)
2. Challenge (1 paragraph, 80 words)
3. Solution (1 paragraph, 100 words)
4. Results (3 bullet points with numbers)
5. Quote from customer (draft for approval)
6. CTA

title: AI 帮我写求职信（cover letter）
Ghostwrite in my voice
AI 校对（找问题不改风格）
body:
校对下面文本。只标出问题，不改风格：

要求：
1. 错别字 / 标点 / 语法（明确性问题）
2. 事实性可疑点（标注「需核实」）
3. 自相矛盾的地方
4. 引用 / 数字格式不统一
5. 不要改动作者的口吻、词汇偏好、句长选择

输出格式：
- 行号 + 原文片段 + 问题分类 + 建议（如有显然修改）

文本：
{{text:multiline=}}

title: AI 改写为「读起来不累」版
body:
下面这段文字读起来很累（长句、术语多、抽象）。改写让它顺畅但保留信息密度：

规则：
- 长句拆短
- 抽象词换具象例子
- 主动语态优先
- 删冗余转折词（"然而""因此""事实上"）
- 不要简化掉关键技术术语
- 段落保持原结构

原文：
{{text:multiline=}}

title: Indie Hackers launch post
body:
Write an Indie Hackers launch post for {{product:string=}}.

Revenue so far: {{revenue:string=}}
Key lesson: {{lesson:string=}}

Requirements:
- Lead with the most counter-intuitive decision you made
- Include specific numbers (MRR, users, churn)
- One section: "What I'd do differently"
- End with a concrete question to the IH community
- No humble-brag framing; be direct

title: 短视频脚本 Hook 测试
body:
为这个内容生成 5 种不同的开场 hook（前 3 秒），目标：滑到的人忍不住停下。

内容主题：{{theme:string=}}
受众：{{audience:string=}}

5 种 hook 风格：
1. 反常识陈述
2. 具体数字 + 对比
3. 第一人称糟糕经历
4. 直接挑战观众认知
5. 神秘感（讲一半留悬念）

每个 hook ≤ 20 字。

title: AI 学术论文快速精读
body:
我要快速理解这篇 {{field:string=}} 领域的论文：{{paper_title:string=}}

请：
1. 用 5 句话概括核心贡献
2. 列 3 个最重要 figure / 公式
3. 解释作者的关键 insight（用普通人能懂的话）
4. 跟我说哪个段落值得深读
5. 类比另一个我可能熟悉的领域帮我理解

title: Literature review outline
body:
I need to write a literature review on {{topic:string=}}.

I have found these papers:
{{papers:multiline=}}

Help me:
1. Group by sub-theme (identify 3-5 clusters)
2. Identify the main debates / tensions between camps
3. Find the "gap" that justifies new research
4. Suggest 3 more papers I should search for (search query form)
5. Draft the opening paragraph of the review

title: Extract key claims from paper
body:
Extract all falsifiable empirical claims from this paper:

{{abstract_or_excerpt:multiline=}}

For each claim:
1. Quote the exact sentence
2. Restate in plain language
3. Rate confidence (direct evidence / inferred / speculation)
4. Note if contradicted by other work you know of
5. Suggest one way to test or replicate

title: Dify 工作流 RAG agent prompt
body:
你正在 RAG 模式下。检索结果在 {{context:multiline=}} 里。

规则：
1. 答案必须直接源于检索片段；不引用 = 不知道
2. 每条断言后用 [片段编号] 标注来源
3. 检索结果矛盾时优先时间最新的，并明示矛盾
4. 检索片段不足以回答时直接说「知识库中无此信息」，不要补脑

用户问题：{{question:string=}}

title: 对比两个大模型（用户视角）
body:
对比 {{model_a:string=Claude}} 和 {{model_b:string=GPT-4o}} 在 {{use_case:string=}} 上：
1. 哪个明显更强？引用最近半年公开 benchmark
2. 价格 / 速度 / 上下文长度差异
3. 哪些场景下 {{model_a}} 是 strict win？反之？
4. 如果用户预算紧 / 不紧，分别推荐哪个

来源不确定的明确说「我不确定」。

title: AI 解读图表 / 数据
body:
下面是数据 / 图表描述：

{{data:multiline=}}

分析：
1. 三个最显著的 trend
2. 三个反常 / outlier 数据点 + 可能原因
3. 哪些断言能从数据直接推出 vs 哪些需要更多数据
4. 一个值得继续追问的子问题
5. 容易误读的地方（warning）

title: 用户访谈半结构化模板
body:
为 {{product_or_idea:string=}} 设计用户访谈剧本。半结构化（≤ 30 分钟）。

输出：
- 暖场 3 分钟：让对方放松的开场（与产品无关）
- 背景挖掘 5 分钟：他在 {{problem_space:string=}} 里现有的工作流是怎样的
- 核心 15 分钟：3-4 个开放问题，每个有 follow-up 提示
- 反向问 5 分钟：让对方教我没想到的
- 收尾 2 分钟

问题原则：用 "上次你 X 是什么时候？讲讲那次" 而不是 "你会 X 吗？"

title: AI 分析竞品
Research outline generator
AI 知识图谱构建
body:
把 {{topic:string=}} 这个主题构建成知识图谱：

要求：
1. 列 10-15 个核心概念
2. 用 mermaid graph 画概念间的关系（依赖 / 因果 / 包含 / 类比）
3. 标注每个概念的「难度（1-5）」和「重要性（1-5）」
4. 推荐学习顺序（拓扑排序）
5. 每个概念给 1 句话定义 + 1 个具体例子

title: AI 整理 PDF / 长文 → Anki 卡
body:
下面是文本 / PDF 抽取内容。生成 Anki 卡片：

规则：
- 每张卡 question/answer 对
- question 短、聚焦一个点（≤ 25 字）
- answer 简洁（≤ 60 字），不复述背景
- 出 8-15 张，不要太多
- 跳过定义性死记硬背的，挑「容易考」的关键概念
- 可加 cloze（{{c1::xxx}} 形式）

文本：
{{text:multiline=}}

title: Japanese → English (natural)
body:
Translate this Japanese text to natural English. Preserve:
- Keigo level (indicate if original is formal/informal)
- Implied cultural context (add brief [bracketed] notes where needed)
- Technical terms verbatim

Do NOT literal-translate set phrases; find their English functional equivalents.

{{text:multiline=}}

title: English → Japanese (business keigo)
Chinese tech doc → English
body:
Translate this Chinese technical document to English for an international developer audience.

{{text:multiline=}}

Rules:
- Keep all code snippets and file paths unchanged
- Translate comments inside code blocks
- Preserve all markdown formatting
- For Chinese-specific concepts (e.g. 备案, 工单), keep the pinyin + add a brief EN explanation in parentheses
- Flag any sentence that might be ambiguous with [REVIEW NEEDED]

title: English → Korean (UX copy)
body:
Translate this UX copy from English to Korean (한국어).

{{text:multiline=}}

Guidelines:
- Use 존댓말 (polite formal) for UI labels
- App button labels: concise (≤ 6 characters preferred)
- Don't transliterate English words if a natural Korean equivalent exists
- Flag any phrase where direct translation would sound unnatural

title: AI 翻译保留语气（non-literal）
body:
翻译这段 {{source_lang:string=英文}} → {{target_lang:string=中文}}。

要求：
- 不要逐字翻译；译出原文的「感觉」
- 习语 / 俚语用目标语言对应表达
- 文化背景词保留原文 + 简短解释（如「年功序列 (seniority-based pay)」）
- 语气强度匹配（讽刺仍讽刺、平静仍平静）
- 末尾给 3 个翻译选择（保守 / 平衡 / 大胆）

原文：
{{text:multiline=}}

title: AI 翻译保留风格（小说 / 散文）
body:
把下面这段 {{source_lang:string=English}} 翻译成 {{target_lang:string=中文}}：

{{text:multiline=}}

要求：
- 保留作者语气（如严肃/俏皮/沉郁）
- 保留比喻和修辞
- 不机翻，意译为主
- 保留专有名词（人名/地名）原文 + 中文音译
- 输出 3 个版本：直译 / 意译 / 文学化

title: AI 写日语商务邮件
Idiom adaptation (cross-language)
body:
Adapt these {{source_lang:string=English}} idioms / expressions to natural {{target_lang:string=Japanese}} equivalents:

{{expressions:multiline=}}

For each:
1. Source idiom
2. Literal translation (so I understand the gap)
3. Natural target-language equivalent
4. Usage context (formal / casual / written / spoken)
5. Example sentence in target language

title: Subtitle / caption localization
body:
Localize these video subtitles from {{source_lang:string=English}} to {{target_lang:string=Japanese}}.

```
{{subtitles:multiline=}}
```

Constraints:
- Each line ≤ {{max_chars:int=28}} characters (reading speed)
- Preserve timing markers [HH:MM:SS] exactly
- Condense without losing meaning if line is too long
- Culture-adapt jokes / references — add [ADAPTED] marker
- Speaker labels (Speaker 1:) stay in source language

title: App strings localization (8 languages)
body:
Localize these app UI strings to all 8 target languages:

Source strings (English):
{{strings:multiline=}}

Target languages: Japanese (ja), Simplified Chinese (zh-Hans), Traditional Chinese (zh-Hant), Korean (ko), Spanish (es), French (fr), German (de)

Rules:
- App name stays as-is
- Button labels: ≤ 10 characters in CJK, ≤ 15 in Latin scripts
- Preserve {{variable}} placeholders exactly
- Flag any string that needs cultural adaptation with [ADAPT:lang]

title: English → 繁體中文 (Taiwan)
body:
Translate this text to Traditional Chinese (繁體中文) for a Taiwan audience.

{{text:multiline=}}

Rules:
- Use Taiwan-standard terminology (not mainland China variants)
  - e.g. 軟體 not 软件, 檔案 not 文件, 網路 not 网络
- Polite register suitable for professional context
- Preserve all technical terms in English if commonly used as-is in Taiwan tech industry
- Flag any term where Taiwan vs. HK usage differs with [TW/HK?]

title: Spanish → English (regional aware)
body:
Translate this Spanish text to English.

Source region: {{region:string=Mexico}} (affects vocabulary choices)

{{text:multiline=}}

Notes:
- Preserve formality level (tuteo vs usted → you vs formal you in EN)
- Note any LATAM vs Spain vocabulary differences where they affect meaning
- Cultural references: keep + brief [EN context] note
- Output 2 versions: US English / UK English if significant differences exist

title: Japanese Business Meeting Summary
Market analysis brief
body:
Write a market analysis for {{market:string=}} targeting {{audience:string=investors}}.

Include:
1. Market size (TAM / SAM / SOM) with sources
2. 3 main competitors + pricing
3. Key trends (2025-2026)
4. Entry barriers
5. Go-to-market angle most suited for a solo founder / small team
6. Red flags that would make this market hard to enter

title: Cold outreach email (B2B)
body:
Write a cold outreach email to {{prospect_role:string=CTO}} at {{company_type:string=SaaS startup}}.

My offer: {{offer:string=}}
Their likely pain: {{pain:string=}}

Requirements:
- Subject line: ≤ 8 words, no clickbait
- Body: ≤ 150 words
- Open with their world, not your product
- One specific ask (15-min call)
- PS line with social proof / number
- No: "Hope this finds you well", "Quick question", "I'll be brief"

title: Proposal draft for client
body:
Draft a project proposal for {{client:string=}} requesting {{deliverable:string=}}.

Context:
- Client problem: {{problem:string=}}
- Proposed solution: {{solution:string=}}
- Timeline: {{timeline:string=}}
- Budget range: {{budget:string=}}

Sections:
1. Executive Summary (150 words)
2. Problem Statement (with their language from the brief)
3. Our Approach (3-phase breakdown)
4. Deliverables + Timeline (table format)
5. Investment (pricing table)
6. Why Us (3 concrete differentiators)
7. Next Steps

title: Meeting agenda + pre-read
body:
Create a meeting agenda and pre-read document for:

Meeting goal: {{goal:string=}}
Duration: {{duration:int=60}} minutes
Attendees: {{attendees:string=}}

Output:
1. Pre-read (2-page max): context, decision needed, options on the table
2. Agenda with time allocations
3. 3 questions each attendee should come prepared to answer
4. Decision template (to fill during meeting)
5. "Not in scope" section to prevent scope creep

title: 商业模式画布快速填
body:
为「{{idea_one_liner:string=}}」填 Business Model Canvas 9 格：

1. Customer Segments
2. Value Propositions
3. Channels
4. Customer Relationships
5. Revenue Streams
6. Key Resources
7. Key Activities
8. Key Partnerships
9. Cost Structure

每格 ≤ 50 字。空着的格子直接说"未确定"，不要硬填。
末尾：1 句话指出哪一格是这个 idea 的最大风险。

title: Revenue model comparison
body:
Compare these monetization models for {{product:string=}}:

Models to evaluate: {{models:string=freemium, subscription, one-time, usage-based}}

For each model:
1. Fit score for this product (1-10) + reasoning
2. Revenue ceiling at 1K / 10K / 100K users
3. Conversion benchmarks (industry typical)
4. Biggest risk
5. Real product example at similar stage

Final recommendation with 1-paragraph rationale.

title: AppStoreConnect 提审说明（review notes）
AI 用户画像生成（基于真实数据）
body:
我有这些用户数据样本：

{{user_data:multiline=}}

请：
1. 聚类成 3-5 个典型 persona
2. 每个 persona 起名字 + 一句话定位
3. 列每个 persona 的：年龄/职业/痛点/触媒/购买力/触发场景
4. 标注哪个 persona ROI 最高
5. 给每个 persona 1 句话产品定位

title: AI 增长 hack 头脑风暴
body:
我的产品 {{product:string=}} 当前 {{stage:string=}} 阶段，目标 {{growth_goal:string=}}。

请头脑风暴：
1. 10 个 zero-budget 增长策略
2. 5 个 paid 增长（按 CAC 排序）
3. 3 个 viral 钩子设计
4. 2 个反共识的增长路径
5. 每个策略：预期 ROI + 试错成本 + 时间窗
6. 推荐第 1 个先试什么 + 为什么

title: AI 自动写跨部门 RFP 回复
AI 1-on-1 反馈起草（员工 review）
body:
我要给员工 {{name:string=}} 做季度 review。

员工概况: {{role:string=}} / 在职 {{tenure:string=}}
本季度产出: {{achievements:multiline=}}
问题: {{issues:string=}}

请起草 review 文稿:
1. 本季亮点 2-3 条具体（非空话）
2. 改进建议 1-2 条（带具体例子）
3. 下季 SMART 目标 3 条
4. 需要公司提供的支持
5. 整体评级 + 简短评语

语气: 直接但温暖, 不要套路话.

title: Founder monthly investor update
StoreKit 2 purchase flow
body:
Implement a complete StoreKit 2 purchase flow in Swift for product ID {{product_id:string=com.example.premium}}.

Requirements:
- `PurchaseState` enum: idle / purchasing / success / failed(String) / cancelled / pending
- 5s timeout on `Product.products(for:)` — surface `.timedOut` state not spinner
- `refreshEntitlements()` via `Transaction.currentEntitlements`
- Drain unfinished transactions at launch
- `@MainActor` throughout
- `restore()` via `AppStore.sync()`
- Every branch must produce visible UI state (no silent failures)

title: SwiftUI animation from spec
Generate test suite from spec
body:
Generate a comprehensive test suite for:

Function/class: {{target:string=}}
Language/framework: {{framework:string=Swift/XCTest}}
Spec:
{{spec:multiline=}}

Include:
- Happy path (1-2 cases)
- Boundary values (min, max, empty, nil)
- Error / exception paths
- Concurrency safety (if async)
- Snapshot tests for UI (if applicable)

Each test: arrange / act / assert pattern. No magic numbers — use named constants.

title: GitHub Actions CI workflow
body:
Write a GitHub Actions CI workflow for {{project_type:string=iOS Swift app}}.

Requirements:
- Trigger: push to main + PR
- Steps: checkout, cache dependencies, build, test, upload artifact
- Matrix: {{matrix:string=iOS 17, iOS 18}}
- Secrets handling: {{secrets:string=API_KEY, CERTIFICATE}}

Output the complete `.github/workflows/ci.yml` file.

title: Architecture decision record (ADR)
body:
Write an Architecture Decision Record for:

Decision: {{decision:string=}}
Context: {{context:multiline=}}
Options considered: {{options:string=}}

ADR format:
## Status: [Proposed / Accepted / Deprecated]
## Context
## Decision
## Consequences (positive + negative + risks)
## Alternatives rejected (why)
## Review date

title: Regex builder + explainer
LLM prompt evaluation framework
body:
Design a systematic evaluation for this prompt:

Prompt under test:
{{prompt:multiline=}}

Task type: {{task_type:string=classification}}

Output:
1. 5 evaluation dimensions (accuracy / consistency / safety / format / latency)
2. 10 test cases (diverse edge cases)
3. Scoring rubric per dimension (0-3 scale)
4. 3 adversarial inputs that might break the prompt
5. Suggested improvements based on anticipated failures

title: Observability instrumentation plan
body:
Design observability for {{service_name:string=}}.

Stack: {{stack:string=}}

Output:
1. Key metrics (what to measure, unit, alert threshold)
2. Structured log schema (JSON fields per log level)
3. Trace spans (what to start/end a span around)
4. Error budget SLO definition
5. Dashboard panels: latency p50/p95/p99, error rate, throughput
6. On-call runbook trigger conditions

title: Security review checklist
body:
Security review this code for {{context:string=iOS app}}:

```{{language:string=Swift}}
{{code:multiline=}}
```

Check for:
1. Input validation / injection risks
2. Authentication / authorization gaps
3. Sensitive data in logs / crash reports
4. Insecure data storage (Keychain vs UserDefaults)
5. Network security (TLS pinning, certificate validation)
6. Third-party dependency vulnerabilities
7. Privacy permission misuse

Rating: Critical / High / Medium / Low for each finding.

title: Refactor toward SOLID principles
body:
Refactor this code toward SOLID:

```{{language:string=Swift}}
{{code:multiline=}}
```

For each principle:
1. **S** — what does this class do too many things?
2. **O** — what would break if requirements change?
3. **L** — any unsafe casts or override violations?
4. **I** — any protocol with unused methods?
5. **D** — what concrete types should be protocols?

Output refactored code + before/after comparison.

title: MCP server 完整骨架（含工具调用）
Continue.dev 项目 system prompt
body:
你是这个 {{language:string=Swift}} 项目的 pair programmer。

上下文优先级（高 → 低）：
1. .continueignore 之外的文件 + 当前打开 tab
2. git diff（未提交改动）
3. 同目录其他文件
4. 项目 README

回答风格：
- 先 short summary
- 后给完整代码块（标语言）
- 最后一行：「需要我跑测试 / 解释这段 / 改某个细节吗？」

title: Aider 单 commit 任务模板
body:
/add {{files:string=}}

任务：{{task:string=}}

约束：
- 一个 logical change，对应一个 commit
- commit message 用 conventional commits（feat/fix/refactor/test/docs/chore）
- 不顺手清理无关代码（即使诱人）
- 完成后跑 {{test_cmd:string=}} 确认

/run pytest -xvs

title: 公众号长文结构 outline
body:
为下面的素材组织成一篇 2000-3000 字的公众号长文：

素材：{{material:multiline=}}
核心论点：{{core_argument:string=}}

输出 outline：
- 标题（≤ 25 字，避开关键词堆砌）
- 开篇钩子 100 字
- 正文 4-5 个段落（每段 1 个分论点 + 1 个具体例证）
- 结尾 80 字（call to think，不是 call to action）
- 末尾 3 个备选标题（不同角度）

title: AI 生成小红书爆款标题
body:
话题：{{topic:string=}}
受众：{{audience:string=}}

生成 10 个适合小红书的标题：
- ≤ 18 字
- 含具体数字 / 对比 / 反常识 三选一
- 不要带 emoji（封面已有）
- 不要「赶紧收藏」这种过气句式
- 末尾不要标点

按预测点击率从高到低排。

title: AI 起视频脚本（5 分钟讲解型）
body:
我要做一个 5 分钟视频，主题 {{topic:string=}}。

受众: {{audience:string=}}
核心 takeaway: {{key_message:string=}}

请生成完整脚本:
0:00-0:15 钩子
0:15-1:00 问题陈述 + 共鸣
1:00-3:30 干货核心 (3 个 sub-section)
3:30-4:30 case + 应用
4:30-5:00 CTA + 下集预告

每段标注镜头 (近/中/远) + B-roll 建议

title: AI 起社交媒体 1 周内容 batch
写邮件回复（直接，单 ask）
dev.to technical article
Medium post (personal essay)
body:
Write a personal essay for Medium about {{topic:string=}}.

Personal angle: {{my_experience:string=}}
Key insight: {{insight:string=}}

Format:
- Opening anecdote (150 words, sensory, specific)
- Bridge to universal theme
- 3 body sections: personal story / broader insight alternating
- Honest, slightly vulnerable closing paragraph
- No subheadings — flowing prose
- Target: 800-1200 words

title: Substack paid issue
body:
Write a paid Substack issue on {{topic:string=}}.

Niche: {{niche:string=}}
Exclusive value for paid subscribers: {{exclusive_value:string=}}

Structure:
- Subject line (3 options)
- Opening (set expectations)
- Main piece (800-1200 words, 2+ concrete examples)
- Members-only deep-dive (300 words: data / template / checklist)
- Closing question to reply to

title: Hacker News Show HN post
body:
Write a Hacker News Show HN post for {{product:string=}}.

Description: {{description:string=}}
Most interesting technical decision: {{tech_decision:string=}}

Requirements:
- Title: "Show HN: [Product] – [one-line description]"
- Lead with the technical problem, not the product
- Mention what is surprising or non-obvious
- Be honest about limitations
- Invite specific feedback
- ≤ 300 words, plain text, no markdown headers

title: User-facing changelog
body:
Write a user-facing changelog for version {{version:string=}}.

Raw changes:
{{changes:multiline=}}

Output:
- Version headline (emoji + version + 1-line theme)
- What's New (user benefits, not technical details)
- Fixed (only user-visible fixes)
- Coming Soon teaser (1 item max, optional)

Tone: friendly, specific, no jargon. ≤ 250 words total.

title: SEO content brief
Indie Hackers launch post
body:
Write an Indie Hackers launch post for {{product:string=}}.

Revenue so far: {{revenue:string=}}
Key lesson: {{lesson:string=}}

Requirements:
- Lead with the most counter-intuitive decision you made
- Include specific numbers (MRR, users, churn)
- One section: "What I'd do differently"
- End with a concrete question to the IH community
- No humble-brag framing; be direct

title: Systematic review protocol
body:
Design a systematic review protocol for:

Research question: {{rq:string=}}
Field: {{field:string=}}

Output:
1. PICO framework (Population / Intervention / Comparison / Outcome)
2. Inclusion / exclusion criteria
3. Search strings for 3 databases (PubMed / Scopus / Google Scholar)
4. Data extraction template (table headers)
5. Quality assessment rubric (5 criteria)
6. PRISMA flow diagram skeleton

title: Paper critique (reviewer style)
body:
Critique this paper as a peer reviewer:

Title: {{title:string=}}
Abstract:
{{abstract:multiline=}}

Review dimensions:
1. Research question clarity and novelty
2. Methodology appropriateness
3. Results presentation
4. Limitations acknowledged vs. hidden
5. Contribution to field
6. Reproducibility

Output reviewer-style comments (Major / Minor / Accept) + decision

title: Market size estimation (TAM/SAM/SOM)
Trend analysis report
body:
Write a trend analysis for {{topic:string=}} in {{year:string=2025-2026}}.

Audience: {{audience:string=product managers}}

Sections:
1. Signal vs. noise (adoption curve framing)
2. 3 leading indicators to watch
3. Impact on {{industry:string=}} (opportunities + threats)
4. Innovators already acting (3 named examples)
5. 12-month forecast (3 scenarios)
6. Sources cited (prioritize primary data)

title: Hypothesis generation from data
body:
I have this dataset / observation:

{{data:multiline=}}

Generate 5 competing hypotheses:
- State each as a falsifiable claim
- Estimate prior probability (1-10)
- Describe what evidence would confirm / disconfirm each
- Identify the cheapest / fastest test
- Flag which hypotheses are mutually exclusive

title: A/B experiment design
body:
Design an A/B experiment for:

Hypothesis: {{hypothesis:string=}}
Key metric: {{metric:string=}}
MDE: {{mde:string=5%}}

Output:
1. Control vs. treatment definition
2. Sample size calculation (power: 80%, alpha: 0.05)
3. Assignment method (user / session / feature-flag)
4. Guard rail metrics (must not degrade)
5. Duration recommendation
6. Analysis plan (test type)
7. Rollout plan if successful

title: Survey design (avoid bias)
Citation chain expansion
body:
I'm reading: {{paper_title:string=}}

Expand my reading list:
1. 5 foundational papers this work likely cites
2. 5 recent papers that likely cite this
3. 3 adjacent field cross-pollination papers
4. Search query templates for Google Scholar / Semantic Scholar
5. Key authors to follow in this area

title: User interview synthesis
body:
Synthesize insights from these user interviews:

{{transcripts:multiline=}}

Analysis:
1. Affinity clustering: group quotes by theme
2. Jobs-to-be-done: what are users hiring this for?
3. Pains vs. gains: unmet needs vs. delights
4. Surprising quotes: what defied our assumptions?
5. Recommended next research questions
6. Confidence level per insight (n=, quote count)

title: Statistical results interpretation
body:
Help me interpret these statistical results:

{{results:multiline=}}

Test used: {{test:string=}}
Sample size: {{n:string=}}

Provide:
1. Plain-language interpretation of p-value / CI
2. Effect size assessment (Cohen's d / r / eta-squared)
3. Practical vs. statistical significance distinction
4. Assumptions check
5. Common misinterpretations to avoid
6. How to report in a paper (APA format)

title: AI 学习路径生成（30 天计划）
body:
我想 30 天内入门 {{topic:string=}}（当前水平 {{current_level:string=初学者}}）。每天 {{hours_per_day:int=2}} 小时。

输出：
- 每周一个里程碑（4 个里程碑）
- 每天具体任务（任务名 + 时长 + 资源链接 if 公开）
- 第 7/14/21/30 天的自检测验（≤ 5 题）
- 容易卡住的 3 个点 + 卡住时怎么求助

title: AI 错题分析 + 知识漏洞定位
body:
我做了 {{subject:string=}} 的题，下面是错题：

{{wrong_answers:multiline=}}

请：
1. 归类错题类型（概念混淆 / 计算 / 粗心 / 知识缺失）
2. 定位每题反映的具体知识点漏洞
3. 推荐每个漏洞 3 个针对性训练
4. 出 5 道类似题让我练习
5. 给我下次怎么避免同类错的 1 句话提醒

title: AI 概念解释 5 层渐进
body:
解释概念 {{concept:string=}}，用 5 层渐进方式：

1. **5 岁懂**：用比喻 + 简单生活例子
2. **15 岁懂**：用基础学科知识 + 1 个例子
3. **大学生懂**：用形式化定义 + 推导
4. **从业者懂**：用实战场景 + 边界情况
5. **专家懂**：用前沿争议 / 未解问题

每层 50-100 字。

title: Industry deep dive (analyst style)
body:
Write an analyst-style deep dive on {{industry:string=}}.

Focus: {{angle:string=competitive dynamics}}

Sections:
1. Industry structure (Porter's Five Forces)
2. Value chain analysis (where is margin captured?)
3. 5 incumbents + 3 challengers (with positioning)
4. Key regulatory / macro risks (2025-2026)
5. Winner-take-all vs. fragmented end-state
6. Where a new entrant could find a wedge
7. Data sources and confidence levels

title: SaaS pricing strategy
body:
Design a pricing strategy for {{product:string=}} ({{stage:string=early stage SaaS}}).

Current pricing: {{current:string=free}}
Competitor pricing: {{competitors:string=}}

Output:
1. Recommended tier structure (Free / Base / Pro / Enterprise)
2. Feature allocation per tier
3. Price points with rationale
4. Annual discount recommendation
5. Freemium paywall trigger
6. Expansion revenue mechanics (seats / usage / add-ons)
7. First 90 days experiment plan

title: AI 起独立开发者每月财务报表
body:
我是独立开发者，本月数据:

收入: {{income_breakdown:string=}} (按 source)
支出: {{expenses:string=}}
时间投入: {{hours_per_project:string=}}

请生成:
1. 1 页月报 (收入/支出/利润/小时单价)
2. 跟上月对比 (% 变化)
3. 高 ROI / 低 ROI 项目识别
4. 下月 3 个具体优化建议
5. 是否值得继续 vs 砍掉的项目判断

title: AI 起社群运营月度 SOP
body:
我运营一个 {{community_type:string=付费知识社群}} ({{member_count:string=}} 人)。

当前问题: {{current_issues:string=}}

请生成月度 SOP:
1. 每天动作 (5-15 min)
2. 每周动作 (1-2 hr)
3. 每月动作 (~5 hr)
4. 续费节点
5. 失活用户挽回 trigger
6. 内容更新节奏
7. 高活跃用户激励 mechanism

title: AI 副业每周节奏规划
body:
我有 {{weekly_hours:int=10}} 小时/周可投副业 {{side_project:string=}}。

现状: {{current_stage:string=}}
90 天目标: {{goal:string=}}

请帮我:
1. 拆 13 周计划 (每周 2-3 个 SMART task)
2. 每周 hour 分配 (production / marketing / admin)
3. 每月里程碑 (3 个)
4. 失败 fallback (30/60/90 天)
5. 我可以放弃的 5 个 nice-to-have

title: AI 广告投放 brief 生成
body:
我要在 {{platform:string=小红书}} 投广告，预算 {{budget:string=}}。

产品：{{product:string=}}
目标受众：{{target:string=}}

生成完整 brief：
1. 3 套创意脚本（各 30 秒）
2. 关键词 + 标签建议
3. 出价策略（CPC/CPM/oCPM 选择）
4. 时段 + 地域 + 兴趣定向
5. A/B test 矩阵
6. ROI 预期 + 何时停

title: AI 内容创作流水线设计
body:
我想做 {{niche:string=}} 方向的 {{platform:string=}} AI 内容账号。设计完整流水线：

1. 选题：每周从哪取？（关键词 / 热搜 / 信息源）
2. 撰稿：哪个 AI 写第一稿？{{writing_ai:string=Claude}}
3. 配图：哪个 AI 出图？{{image_ai:string=Midjourney}}
4. 校对：人工 + AI 双重
5. 发布：每周几篇 / 哪个时间段
6. 数据回收：3 周后总结

附：每个工具粗略时间成本和金钱成本。

title: AI 业务数据深度分析
body:
我有这份 {{data_type:string=}} 数据：

{{data:multiline=}}

请：
1. 找出 3 个最反常的趋势
2. 用同期/环比/RFM 分类
3. 推断背后的业务原因
4. 给 5 个可执行的优化建议（按 ROI 排序）
5. 哪些需要进一步数据验证
6. 输出可直接给老板看的 1 页摘要

title: Partnership / collab pitch
French → English (register aware)
German → English (technical)
body:
Translate this German technical text to English.

Domain: {{domain:string=engineering}}

{{text:multiline=}}

Rules:
- Technical terms: use established English equivalents from the {{domain}} field
- Compound nouns: break into readable EN phrases
- Passive voice common in DE tech writing: convert to active voice in EN where natural
- Flag untranslatable terms with [DE: original]

title: API error messages localization
body:
Localize these API error messages for a {{target_lang:string=Japanese}} audience.

Error messages (English):
{{messages:multiline=}}

Rules:
- Be specific but not alarming (not "fatal error" — say what to do next)
- Match platform conventions (iOS / Android / Web)
- Keep technical codes (ERR_404) intact; localize the description only
- Output as JSON: {{"original": ..., "localized": ...}} per message

title: English → 繁體中文 (Taiwan)
body:
Translate to Traditional Chinese (繁體中文) for a Taiwan audience.

{{text:multiline=}}

Rules:
- Use Taiwan-standard terminology:
  e.g. 軟體 not 软件, 檔案 not 文件, 網路 not 网络
- Polite register for professional context
- Keep technical terms in English if commonly used as-is in Taiwan
- Flag any term where Taiwan vs. HK usage differs with [TW/HK?]

title: English → Spanish (LATAM)
body:
Translate to Latin American Spanish.

Target country: {{country:string=Mexico}}

{{text:multiline=}}

Notes:
- Use vosotros-free forms (use ustedes for plural you)
- Prefer LATAM vocabulary over Castilian Spanish
  e.g. computadora not ordenador, celular not móvil
- For UI/app copy: match iOS/Android Spanish style guides
- Flag Spain vs LATAM differences with [ES/LATAM?]

title: Translation quality review
body:
Review this translation for quality:

Source ({{source_lang:string=English}}):
{{source:multiline=}}

Translation ({{target_lang:string=Japanese}}):
{{translation:multiline=}}

Rate and flag:
1. Accuracy (meaning lost or distorted?)
2. Fluency (does it read naturally in target language?)
3. Terminology consistency
4. Omissions or additions not in source
5. Register match

Output: Issue list (Critical / Minor) + corrected version
